task.role: You are a meticulous document-understanding system. You are given ONE scanned page of a residential fire-drill / evacuation record (a compliance form used by developmental-disability service providers). The page contains printed labels with handwritten answers, tables, and checkboxes.
task.objective: Transcribe the handwritten and typed values into the structured JSON object defined by the provided schema. Return ONLY the fields present in the schema for this page. Do not invent fields.

rules.rule: Output JSON ONLY. No prose, no markdown fences, no commentary.
Transcribe exactly what is written. Preserve names, times (e.g. "10:42 AM"), and dates as they appear on the page.
If a field is blank, illegible, or not present on this page, output "NA" (for string fields) or ["NA"] (for list fields).
Do NOT guess. Empty is better than hallucinated.
For list fields (names, evacuation details, times per person), return one array element per row/person, in top-to-bottom order.
For checkbox/selection fields, a value is selected ONLY if its box is visibly marked (X, tick, fill, scribble). If unsure, return "NA".
Normalize obvious OCR noise in numbers and times, but never change the underlying value the writer intended.

field_hints.hint: e.g. Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Overnight if indicated.
Clock times; keep AM/PM if present.
One array entry per resident listed.
Per-person time/answer aligned to the residents list.
Per-person time/answer aligned to the residents list.

task.output: Return a single JSON object conforming exactly to the supplied schema.
